criteria.criterion: Two goroutines must never simultaneously believe they are the sole valid split-executor for the same fileID (the key correctness property from the verification finding)
criteria.mutation_evidence: Inverting reclaimIfExpired's expiry check (forcing every check to appear expired) makes this test fail -- confirms it actually exercises the guard-preservation invariant, not just a happy path.

criteria.criterion: reclaimIfExpired's check-then-act sequence must be fenced: a reclaim decision must not proceed to mutate state if the legitimate holder already completed by the time of the actual action
covered_by: TestAbandonedSplittingRecoversAfterTimeout (EndSplit after reclaim returns ErrNotSplitting rather than corrupting a fresh generation)
criteria.mutation_evidence: Same inversion as V1 breaks TestAbandonedSplittingRecoversAfterTimeout too (reclaim fires too early, before the documented expiry point).

criteria.criterion: Lease-timeout reclaim still unblocks AdmitWrite callers (writers) even though the guard itself is no longer auto-released
EndSplit's lease-clearing must have an observable, tested effect (the disclosed coverage gap)
criteria.mutation_evidence: Disabling EndSplit's delete(o.leases, fileID) line makes exactly this test fail (direct white-box inspection of the leases map immediately after EndSplit), while TestAbandonedSplittingRecoversAfterTimeout and TestReclaimNeverDoubleAcquiresGuardForSlowHolder still pass under this same mutation -- confirms this test closes the specific gap the verifier's mutation #2 found, without depending on the other two tests to catch it.

criteria.criterion: No regression in pre-existing behavior/tests (double-split refusal, AdmitWrite gating, MVCC-reader isolation, guard registry bounding, WAL-before-apply crash safety, split_race_test.go's real concurrent race test)
criteria.evidence: go test ./engine/split/... -race: all tests pass, including the untouched split_race_test.go and execute_test.go/execute.go-adjacent tests

criteria.criterion: Package still compiles with BeginSplit/EndSplit/AbortSplit signatures unchanged (split_race_test.go, out of scope, depends on this)
WithClock/WithLeaseDuration API surface reduced to match cited precedent

residual_gap_disclosed_not_closed.description: A fileID whose true split holder has genuinely crashed (never calls EndSplit/AbortSplit at all) has its guard permanently stuck after a reclaim: future BeginSplit calls keep returning ErrAlreadySplitting indefinitely, reintroducing part of the original pre-4.5.3.3 gap this subtask set out to close. Writers remain unblocked (AdmitWrite succeeds) regardless.
residual_gap_disclosed_not_closed.why_not_closed_in_this_fix_cycle: Closing it without reopening the double-acquisition hole requires either (a) an ownership/fencing token added to FileGuard.TryAcquire/Release (engine/split/guard.go, explicitly out of this fix-cycle's file scope -- ARCHITECTURE-DISCOVERY.md documents why), or (b) threading such a token through BeginSplit/EndSplit's own signatures (blocked by engine/split/split_race_test.go's existing calls with today's fileID-only signatures, which this fix-cycle cannot touch).
residual_gap_disclosed_not_closed.recommendation: A follow-up subtask scoped to include guard.go (and guard_test.go) should add a caller-opaque fencing token to FileGuard.TryAcquire (returned) and FileGuard.Release (required), then thread it through BeginSplit's return value and EndSplit's parameters, updating split_race_test.go's call sites in the same change.
